docs.title: CrisisSignal AI
docs.text:
Trustworthy, multilingual and multimodal crisis intelligence from noisy social streams.

CrisisSignal AI is a portfolio-grade research and engineering platform for trustworthy crisis-message analysis. The implemented core provides a reproducible classical baseline, uncertainty-aware routing, an API, monitoring utilities and extension points for deep learning, transformers, LLMs and multimodal evidence.

docs.title: Persistent identifiers
docs.text:
• Software release v1.0.0 DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21463318
  • Companion-book DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21459117

The software and book are related but distinct scholarly records.

docs.title: Research and engineering scope
docs.text:
• Reproducible classical NLP baselines.
  • Duplicate-aware dataset splitting.
  • Probability calibration and uncertainty-aware abstention.
  • Human-review routing for low-confidence predictions.
  • FastAPI, command-line and dashboard interfaces.
  • Monitoring, drift detection and audit-oriented evaluation.
  • Extension points for recurrent networks, transformers, LLMs and multimodal evidence.

docs.title: Responsible use
docs.text: The system is intended for research, teaching and carefully supervised decision support. It is not an emergency authority and must not be used as the sole basis for dispatch, medical triage, evacuation, law-enforcement or public-warning decisions.

docs.title: Companion book
docs.text:
Engineering Trustworthy Crisis Intelligence: From Noisy Social Streams to Multilingual, Multimodal and Human-in-the-Loop Disaster AI

Companion-book DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21459117

The book DOI is separate from the archived software-release DOI 10.5281/zenodo.21463318.

docs.title: Faramarz Kowsari
docs.text: Faramarz Kowsari is an author, Software Engineer and AI researcher based in Istanbul. Focusing on the intersection of technology, education, and personal growth, he has published over 80 digital titles on international platforms. His areas of expertise span Artificial Intelligence, prompt engineering, modern trading strategies (Smart Money Concepts & algorithmic trading), as well as classical literature and mindfulness. In addition to writing, he develops web-based educational tools and creates specialized instructional video content.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    S[Archived or licensed stream] --> V[Schema validation]
    V --> N[Unicode and social-text normalization]
    N --> D[Duplicate-aware grouping]
    D --> M[Model family]
    M --> P[Probability]
    P --> C[Calibration and abstention]
    C --> O[Prediction or human review]
    O --> A[API, batch reports, dashboard]
    A --> R[Audit metadata and drift monitoring]

docs.title: Boundaries
docs.text: The package separates model code from data acquisition, API serving and user interfaces. Optional heavy dependencies are isolated. The default installation remains lightweight and testable on a CPU.

docs.title: Artifact contract
docs.text:
A serialized artifact contains:

{"pipeline": fitted_sklearn_pipeline, "metadata": reproducibility_metadata}

Metadata includes the dataset fingerprint, split parameters, software versions and risk-aware evaluation report.

docs.title: Author, Software Engineer and AI Researcher

Faramarz Kowsari is an author, Software Engineer and AI researcher based in Istanbul. In addition to writing, he develops web-based educational tools and creates specialized instructional video content.

Faramarz Kowsari is the creator and principal maintainer of CrisisSignal AI, a research and engineering platform for trustworthy, multilingual and multimodal crisis intelligence.

docs.title: Persistent identifiers
docs.text:
• Companion book DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21459117
  • Software DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21463318

The book DOI and software DOI identify separate scholarly objects and must not be substituted for one another.

docs.title: Dataset card template
docs.text:
Every dataset added to an experiment should document:

  • canonical name and source;
  • license and redistribution status;
  • collection period and event coverage;
  • languages and geographic distribution;
  • annotation protocol and annotator information;
  • label definitions and class balance;
  • duplicate rate and leakage controls;
  • privacy review and redaction policy;
  • known gaps, biases and excluded populations;
  • whether examples are human-authored, translated or synthetic.

docs.title: Local API
docs.text:
crisis-signal train --data data/train.csv --output artifacts/baseline.joblib
export CRISIS_SIGNAL_MODEL=artifacts/baseline.joblib
uvicorn crisis_signal.api:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
docker compose up --build

The container expects a read-only model artifact mounted at /app/artifacts/baseline.joblib.

docs.title: Production controls still required
docs.text:
• authenticated access and rate limiting;
  • encrypted transport and secret management;
  • immutable model and data version identifiers;
  • audit logging with privacy controls;
  • rollback and incident-response procedures;
  • load, latency and failure-injection testing;
  • domain-expert approval before high-impact use.

docs.title: Companion book
docs.text:
The related companion publication is:

Engineering Trustworthy Crisis Intelligence: From Noisy Social Streams to Multilingual, Multimodal and Human-in-the-Loop Disaster AI

  • Book DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21459117

docs.title: Relationship policy
docs.text:
The software and book are separate scholarly objects:

  • the software DOI identifies executable source code and the archived release;
  • the book DOI identifies the companion publication;
  • neither DOI should be substituted for the other;
  • the Zenodo records should be linked using complementary relations such as isSupplementTo and isSupplementedBy.

Future software releases should receive their own version-specific Zenodo DOI while preserving explicit links to the GitHub tag and the companion book.

docs.title: Ethics, safety and limitations
docs.text: Crisis data can expose vulnerable people. Public availability does not remove ethical obligations.

docs.title: Prohibited claims
docs.text: Do not describe this repository as a verified emergency-response product, a truth detector or an autonomous dispatcher.

docs.title: Known limitations
docs.text:
• Binary labels compress complex humanitarian situations.
  • Social streams are not representative samples of affected populations.
  • Language and connectivity inequalities create systematic blind spots.
  • Adversarial posts, rumors and recycled images can manipulate predictions.
  • Confidence scores are model estimates, not guarantees.
  • Archived benchmarks may not represent current platform behavior.

docs.title: Human oversight
docs.text: High-impact outputs require trained reviewers, corroborating evidence and accountable operational procedures. The review label is a first-class system outcome, not a failure mode.

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text: Crisis classification is asymmetric: missing a real urgent report can be more costly than reviewing a false alarm. The repository therefore reports a configurable cost-weighted error in addition to standard classification metrics.

docs.title: Selective prediction
docs.text:
Messages with probabilities inside an abstention band around the decision threshold are labeled review. This creates a coverage–quality trade-off:

  • wider abstention band: fewer automatic decisions, usually higher selective accuracy;
  • narrower band: more coverage, more risk.

docs.title: Required future evaluations
docs.text:
• cross-event generalization;
  • cross-language transfer;
  • calibration by language and event type;
  • robustness to spelling, code-switching and paraphrase;
  • latency and memory on target hardware;
  • subgroup and geographic error analysis.

docs.title: Intended use
docs.text: Research baseline for binary disaster-message classification and demonstration of reproducible, risk-aware inference.

docs.title: Not intended for
docs.text: Autonomous dispatch, medical triage, evacuation orders, policing, immigration enforcement or identifying individuals.

docs.title: Training data
docs.text: User-supplied CSV with text and binary target. No training dataset is bundled.

docs.title: Model
docs.text: Word and bigram TF-IDF features with class-balanced logistic regression.

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Probability of the disaster class and one of three decisions: disaster, not_disaster, review.

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1, macro F1, Brier score, ECE, coverage, selective accuracy and weighted error cost.

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text: Vocabulary drift, multilingual performance, sarcasm, event shift and intentional misinformation require separate evaluation.

docs.title: Portfolio evidence map
docs.text:
This repository is designed to make engineering and research evidence easy to verify.

Capability Evidence Python package design src/crisis_signal/ and pyproject.toml Data-quality thinking data.py, dataset card and duplicate-aware split ML baselines models/classical.py, training.py Deep-learning breadth optional BiLSTM and transformer adapters Trustworthy AI calibration, abstention and weighted error cost API engineering api.py, schemas and API tests MLOps DVC pipeline, MLflow adapter, Docker and CI Monitoring PSI and token-distribution drift Multimodal foundations image hashing and duplicate detection Research integrity benchmark evidence status and reproducibility protocol Teaching potential book outline, documentation and modular experiments

A recruiter or academic reviewer should be able to distinguish implemented features, historical evidence and planned research without guessing.

docs.title: Reproducibility protocol
docs.text:
1. Record the data source, license and download date.
  2. Calculate a content fingerprint after validation.
  3. Group exact and normalized duplicates before splitting.
  4. Record random seeds and split policy.
  5. Save configuration, package versions and artifact metadata.
  6. Keep historical metrics separate from reproduced runs.
  7. Report error analysis and limitations with every promoted model.
  8. Prefer event-held-out evaluation when event identifiers exist.

The baseline trainer writes a sidecar *.metadata.json file so results can be inspected without deserializing the model.

docs.title: Phase 1 — Reproducible foundations
docs.text:
• Reproduce the original LSTM result.
  • Establish classical baselines on a fixed split.
  • Add data cards and event-aware splits.

docs.title: Phase 2 — Modern encoders
docs.text:
• Benchmark DistilBERT, RoBERTa, DeBERTa and XLM-R.
  • Compare full fine-tuning with LoRA/QLoRA where appropriate.
  • Publish ablation and calibration studies.

docs.title: Phase 3 — Multilingual crisis intelligence
docs.text:
• Evaluate English, Turkish, Persian, Arabic and Spanish.
  • Measure cross-lingual transfer and code-switching robustness.
  • Separate translated, synthetic and human-authored examples.

docs.title: Phase 4 — Multimodal and evidence-aware systems
docs.text:
• Add CrisisMMD-compatible text-image pipelines.
  • Detect duplicate and recycled imagery.
  • Evaluate evidence retrieval only where retrieval is task-relevant.

docs.title: Phase 5 — Human-in-the-loop operations
docs.text:
• Build a review queue and annotation interface.
  • Measure reviewer workload, coverage and error cost.
  • Add drift alerts, incident playbooks and external validation.
